verdict_reasoning: LYN is a Src-family tyrosine kinase that is N-terminally myristoylated and palmitoylated, anchoring it to the inner leaflet of the plasma membrane under baseline conditions — classically cytoplasmic/inner-leaflet. However, multiple studies document that LYN (and other Src-family kinases) can appear on the outer leaflet of cancer cells as an ecto-kinase, consistent with cell-state-induced (oncogenic transformation) surface display. This documented cancer-cell ecto-pool qualifies as contextual/cell_state_induced. The canonical localization is inner-leaflet, but the non-baseline ecto-form on tumor cells is the surface story.
